convergence.target: Percolation Threshold Theory x T Cell Infiltration in Solid Tumors

H8.title: TGF-beta Autocrine Signaling Constitutes Bond-Correlated Percolation That Shifts p_c

clinical_trials.title: Bintrafusp Alfa (M7824) Phase 1 in Solid Tumors
clinical_trials.note: Bifunctional TGF-beta trap + anti-PD-L1; tests simultaneous TGF-beta blockade + immune checkpoint inhibition in immune-excluded tumors. TGF-beta is the causal driver of correlated LOX activation in H8.

clinical_trials.title: Bintrafusp Alfa + Chemotherapy in Stage IV NSCLC

patents.title: Prodrugs of lysyl oxidase inhibitors
patents.assignee: Institute of Cancer Research, Royal Cancer Hospital
patents.note: Explicitly claims LOX inhibition improves T cell migration and anti-PD-1 efficacy. TGF-beta/LOX axis central to H8.

partial_confirmations.claim: TGF-beta regulates LOX/LOXL2 activity, which drives collagen alignment and CD8+ T cell immune exclusion; inhibiting LOXL2 reverses exclusion
partial_confirmations.paper: Bai et al. 2025: GPR4 promotes immune exclusion in colon cancer through LOXL2-mediated extracellular matrix remodeling. Nature Communications.

H8.summary: TGF-beta/LOX/collagen alignment axis directly confirmed in Nature Communications 2025 (PMID 41436455): GPR4 activates JAK2/STAT3/LOXL2/TGF-beta to produce aligned collagen that blocks CD8+ T cells; LOXL2 inhibition reverses immune exclusion. Multiple bintrafusp alfa trials (bifunctional anti-PD-L1 + TGF-beta trap) are active in solid tumors. Patent WO2024003558A1 covers LOX inhibitor prodrugs for immunotherapy combination.

H4.title: Collagen I/III Ratio Acts as a Lattice Topology Switch That Shifts p_c

clinical_trials.title: Phase 1 Study of PRTH-101 (Anti-DDR1) Alone or in Combination with Pembrolizumab in Advanced Solid Tumors
clinical_trials.note: PRTH-101 disrupts DDR1-mediated collagen fiber ALIGNMENT (not just stiffness/density) to restore disordered topology and allow T cell infiltration. Phase 1c enrolled first patient September 2024. Up to 270 patients. Pivotal Phase 2 planned 2026. This trial directly operationalizes the collagen topology switch in H4.

patents.title: Prodrugs of lysyl oxidase inhibitors
patents.assignee: Institute of Cancer Research, Royal Cancer Hospital

partial_confirmations.claim: Collagen III-rich intermingled network topology supports T cell infiltration; collagen I-rich aligned topology restricts T cell access; the network TYPE (I vs III) determines immune permissiveness
partial_confirmations.paper: Fusilier et al. 2026: Macrophages restrict tumor immune infiltration by controlling collagen topography. Science Immunology 11(117): eadw8291.

partial_confirmations.claim: Collagen type I specifically limits CD8+ T cell infiltration and NK cell infiltration in pancreatic cancer; Col1a1 deletion increases CD8/CD4 ratio
partial_confirmations.paper: Preprint: Collagen type I promotes pancreatic tumor growth and limits immune cell infiltration. bioRxiv January 2026.

partial_confirmations.claim: DDR1-mediated collagen fiber ALIGNMENT (not density) creates the physical barrier for immune exclusion; disrupting alignment via anti-DDR1 antibody restores T cell infiltration without changing stiffness
partial_confirmations.paper: PMID 37328286: A highly selective humanized DDR1 mAb reverses immune exclusion by disrupting collagen fiber alignment in breast cancer (2023). Also: PRTH-101 Phase 1 data (PMC12529548).

partial_confirmations.claim: ECM topology (not just density) controls T cell infiltration; collagen topology predicts immune cell localization better than density; collagen I and III have opposite effects on network permissiveness
partial_confirmations.paper: Oncotarget 2024: Understanding the interplay between extracellular matrix topology and tumor-immune interactions.

H4.summary: H4 is the best-converged hypothesis. The Fusilier 2026 Science Immunology paper (PMID 41860994, published same month as session) independently confirms that collagen III creates permissive intermingled networks while collagen I creates restrictive aligned barriers, exactly the topology switch H4 describes. The active Phase 1c trial of PRTH-101 (NCT05753722, anti-DDR1) directly tests disruption of the collagen alignment topology to enable T cell infiltration in combination with pembrolizumab — this is H4's therapeutic implication expressed without percolation language.

H1.title: LOX Crosslink Density as Bond Occupation Probability — Universal Immune Exclusion Threshold at p ~ 0.21-0.25

clinical_trials.title: PXS-5505 (Pan-LOX Inhibitor) + Atezolizumab + Bevacizumab in Hepatocellular Carcinoma
clinical_trials.note: Withdrawn, but the preclinical rationale is directly relevant: pan-LOX inhibition reduces collagen crosslinking, improving T cell migration and anti-PD-L1 efficacy. Phase 1/IIa PXS-5505 myelofibrosis trial completed with positive results 2025 (PMID 40241543).

patents.title: Prodrugs of lysyl oxidase inhibitors
patents.assignee: Institute of Cancer Research, Royal Cancer Hospital

patents.title: Uses of a lysyl oxidase-like 2 inhibitor

partial_confirmations.claim: LOX-mediated collagen crosslinking is the physical basis for T cell exclusion; LOX inhibition combined with anti-PD-1 produces supra-additive immune infiltration
partial_confirmations.paper: Nicolas-Boluda 2021 eLife (ALREADY IN QG — excluded). Supporting new evidence: Bai 2025 Nat Commun (PMID 41436455) shows LOXL2 (same enzyme family) drives the same mechanism in colon cancer. Nature Cancer 2023 PXS-5505 paper shows pan-LOX inhibitor enhances chemotherapy in PDAC via ECM normalization.

partial_confirmations.claim: T cells prefer low-density (low crosslink) collagen regions and are excluded from high-density (high crosslink) regions
partial_confirmations.paper: Migration of CTLs in 3D Collagen Matrices. Biophysical Journal 119:2441 (2020). PMC7732778. T cells distinguish pore sizes and prefer paths of least resistance; porosity controls velocity.

H1.summary: Three independent lines: (1) LOX inhibitor prodrug patent from ICR (WO2024003558A1) explicitly claims immunotherapy combination application citing T cell migration improvement; (2) LOXL2 confirmed as the molecular effector of collagen-mediated immune exclusion in colon cancer (Bai 2025 Nat Commun); (3) pan-LOX inhibitor (PXS-5505) has reached Phase 1b/2 clinical testing in combination with atezolizumab. The specific percolation threshold prediction (p ~ 0.21-0.25) remains untested.

H2.title: Correlation Length Exponent nu=0.88 Predicts T Cell Cluster Size Distribution Near the Hot-Cold Tumor Boundary

partial_confirmations.claim: T cell MSD in 3D collagen matrices is measurable and shows anomalous (sub-diffusive) dynamics that depend on collagen crosslinking/density; anomalous exponents are extractable from tracking data
partial_confirmations.paper: Migration of Cytotoxic T Lymphocytes in 3D Collagen Matrices. Biophysical Journal 119(12):2441-2452 (2020). PMC7732778.
partial_confirmations.note: Confirms the experimental readout (MSD tracking, anomalous exponent extraction) proposed in H2 is technically feasible and has been demonstrated in reconstituted collagen systems.

H2.summary: The specific cluster-size power-law prediction unique to H2 (T cell cluster sizes near hot-cold tumor boundary following a power-law with exponent tau=2.19) has no direct convergence signal — no papers measuring this distribution were found. However, the sub-mechanism (T cell anomalous diffusion in collagen matrices is measurable and depends on crosslinking) is confirmed by Biophys J 2020. Moderate convergence: measurement framework is feasible, but no independent research has targeted the specific cluster-size observable yet.

H7.title: Pan-cancer Universality Class Critical Exponents Are Tumor-Type-Invariant

partial_confirmations.claim: Collagen-mediated immune exclusion is a general mechanism across multiple cancer types (pan-cancer relevance)
partial_confirmations.paper: Multiple reviews confirm pan-cancer relevance: Oncotarget 2024 review (PMC11546212) discusses ECM topology/T cell interactions across lung, pancreatic, breast cancers. Fusilier 2026 Sci Immunol confirms collagen topography mechanism in both mouse models AND human tumors across types.

H7.summary: The pan-cancer universality prediction is the most speculative hypothesis. General convergence that collagen-mediated immune exclusion occurs across cancer types, but no independent research has measured critical exponents across tumor types to test invariance. WEAK convergence.

H5.title: LOX Inhibitor Dose-Response Follows Order Parameter Scaling P_inf ~ (p - p_c)^0.417

partial_confirmations.claim: LOX inhibitor dose-response in vivo shows non-linear (non-monotonic) relationship between dose and immune infiltration improvement, with threshold below which effect is minimal
partial_confirmations.paper: Nature Cancer 2023: A first-in-class pan-lysyl oxidase inhibitor impairs stromal remodeling and enhances gemcitabine response and survival in pancreatic cancer (PXS-5505). The dose-response relationship for collagen normalization was documented but not analyzed for power-law scaling.
partial_confirmations.note: Confirms threshold-like behavior exists (below certain doses, effect is minimal) but does not use percolation scaling language. The specific beta=0.417 exponent prediction is untested.

H5.summary: The dose-response mechanism (LOX inhibition reduces crosslinking, which increases T cell infiltration) is confirmed. However, no study has analyzed the functional form of this relationship for power-law scaling with the percolation exponent beta=0.417. Moderate convergence: mechanism is real, quantitative form is untested.

H3.title: Finite-Size Scaling of T Cell MSD Explains Discordant Infiltration Scores Between Core Biopsies and Resection Specimens

partial_confirmations.claim: Spatial heterogeneity of immune infiltration scores within single tumors is clinically documented and creates biopsy discordance problems
partial_confirmations.paper: Spatially variant immune infiltration scoring in human cancer tissues. npj Precision Oncology 2022. Also: multiple 2024-2025 spatial omics studies confirm intratumoral heterogeneity of immune infiltration is a major problem for biomarker assessment.
partial_confirmations.note: Confirms the clinical PROBLEM that H3 aims to explain (biopsy discordance due to spatial sampling of an inhomogeneous field near a phase transition), but no study has applied finite-size scaling to explain it.

H3.summary: The clinical phenomenon H3 explains (biopsy sampling discordance for immune infiltration scores) is well-documented. However, no independent research has applied finite-size scaling or any percolation analysis to explain this discordance. WEAK convergence: problem confirmed, mechanism not independently tested.

aggregate.ees_justification: Dominant signal: active Phase 1c clinical trial (NCT05753722) targeting collagen topology to reverse immune exclusion. Three PASS hypotheses (H8, H4, H1) all show CONVERGENT_STRONG based on independent molecular confirmation (Fusilier 2026 Sci Immunol, Bai 2025 Nat Commun) plus active commercial investment. EES=8/10.

key_new_papers.title: A highly selective humanized DDR1 mAb reverses immune exclusion by disrupting collagen fiber alignment in breast cancer

key_clinical_trial.mechanism: Anti-DDR1 antibody disrupts collagen fiber alignment to reverse immune exclusion, combined with anti-PD-1
key_clinical_trial.direct_convergence: H4 — collagen topology switch; H1 — LOX-mediated crosslinking threshold
